title: Unexpected results when comparing `NULL` values
description:
### What happens?

Consider the following test case. The second and third queries are expected to return an empty table. However, one row is returned.


### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE  TABLE  t0( c1 INT );
INSERT INTO t0(c1) VALUES (NULL);

SELECT * FROM t0; -- NULL
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE ((((t0.c1)<=(t0.c1))) IS NULL); -- NULL

-- Unexpected behavior
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE t0.c1 <= t0.c1; -- NULL
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE NOT (t0.c1 <= t0.c1); -- NULL 
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 22.04

### DuckDB Version:

v1.3.0-dev2743 0069af20ab

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:
